summary: Fabric v2.0 rc.2 — Implement archive write-half of the knowledge cycle: fab_extract_knowledge MCP tool (server service + tool registration with idempotency on (source_session,type,slug)), fabric-archive Skill template (verbatim layer heuristic + 5-type extraction prompt + decision tree), single cross-platform archive-hint.cjs Stop-hook script + 2 client configs (Claude Code .claude/settings.json + Codex .codex/hooks.json — Cursor explicitly out of scope for Stop-hook surface), wired through fabric init bootstrap stage and fabric hooks command. Closes WRITE half; rc.3 fab_review will close the READ/promote half.
approach: Schemas are pre-locked in commits a0612a9 (api-contracts) + e3975b5 (event-ledger), so the three artefacts (server impl / skill md / hook script) can fan out in Wave 1 in parallel. Wave 2 lays down hook configs (depends only on hook-script path constant) then init+hooks command wiring (deepMerge gets array-append-with-dedupe special case for hooks.Stop[]). Wave 3 covers integration tests + self-repo dogfood in parallel. Wave 4 is a single batched-end Gemini review + per-file ≥90% coverage gate (per-file parser script over coverage-summary.json — does NOT bump vitest.config thresholds which would fail existing files). One atomic commit per task with conventional commit messages; agent (Claude Code) is the executor; convergence + tests authored by agent; review batched once at end.
estimated_time: 12-16 hours (8 tasks × ~1.5-2h each, including coverage iteration in TASK-008)

design_decisions.decision: Idempotency key = sha256({source_session, type, slug}) — triple-keyed, NOT content-addressed
design_decisions.rationale: Including LLM-regenerated user_messages_summary in the hash makes the key brittle (any summary tweak triggers duplicate write). When same triple hits with divergent content, append evidence section to existing pending file rather than overwrite — preserves history while staying idempotent.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Two genuinely different topics under same triple would conflate — accepted because slug is meant to be semantically unique within (type, source_session)

design_decisions.decision: Pending entries have NO `id:` frontmatter (Q2 late-bind)
design_decisions.rationale: Stable_id allocation is rc.3 fab_review approve concern, not rc.2 proposal concern. Matches schema (event-ledger.ts:179 marks stable_id optional) and discussion-followup.md L72 explicit rule.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Pending files cannot be referenced by stable id until promoted — accepted as inherent to the late-bind design

design_decisions.decision: Single archive-hint.cjs script across both clients (NOT per-client variants)
design_decisions.rationale: Verified at templates/{claude,codex}-hooks/fabric-*-reminder.cjs that both Claude Stop and Codex Stop accept identical stdout JSON shape {decision:'block', reason}. discussion.md L451 explicitly locks single-script approach.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Path constants ('.fabric/events.jsonl', event_type literals) hardcoded — DRY violation accepted because hook runs without node_modules access in user repos

design_decisions.decision: Cursor explicitly OUT of scope for rc.2 Stop-hook (documented as schema deviation from original handoff)
design_decisions.rationale: resolver.ts:139 declares Cursor hook:false; no documented Cursor Stop-hook API as of 2026-05. Skipping is honest; ships rc.2 with 2 working clients instead of stalling on Cursor research.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Cursor users get fabric-archive skill installation but no proactive Stop-hook reminder — documented in planning-context.md and dogfood-report.md

design_decisions.decision: Bootstrap stage repurposed for skill install (NOT new InitStageName)
design_decisions.rationale: init.ts:767-775 is a vacated v2 slot. Adding a new 'skills' stage would require updating InitStageName enum + InitStagePlan union + wizard flow + capability table — disproportionate churn for the same outcome.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Bootstrap name no longer literal — accepted because v2 already deleted v1 bootstrap concerns

design_decisions.decision: deepMerge extended with optional arrayAppendPaths option (NOT inline merge in hooks-writer)
design_decisions.rationale: Reusable extension keeps hooks-writer.ts pure to its config-writer responsibility. Backward-compatible (no option = current REPLACE behavior).
design_decisions.tradeoff: deepMerge signature grows by one optional arg — accepted, alternative was duplicating recursion logic in writer module

design_decisions.decision: Per-file ≥90% coverage gate via external parser script (NOT vitest.config global threshold bump)
design_decisions.rationale: Bumping vitest.config thresholds 75→90 would fail existing files immediately. Allowlist parser script targets only rc.2 new code.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Allowlist drift risk for rc.3+ — mitigated by keeping ALLOWLIST visible at top of script with comment 'update per release'

design_decisions.decision: Gemini Review batched at end (TASK-008), NOT per-task
design_decisions.rationale: Per user MEMORY (feedback_review_batching: 'for multi-task lite-plan chains, run Gemini review + coverage ONCE at end, not per-task').
design_decisions.tradeoff: Late discovery of cross-task issues if any — mitigated by detailed convergence criteria per task

checks.completeness: All 11 acceptance criteria from handoff mapped to convergence criteria across TASK-001..TASK-008
checks.granularity: 8 tasks each ~1.5-2h scope; Wave structure groups parallel-safe work
checks.dependencies: DAG validated: Wave1 [001,002,003] → 004 → 005 → Wave3 [006,007] → 008. No circular deps. cli_execution strategies (new/resume/fork/merge_fork) computed correctly
checks.convergence_criteria: All criteria quantified or schema-checkable (line-counts, sha256 equality, deep-equal against fixtures, ≥90% pct, exact text match for verbatim heuristic, list-cardinality)
checks.implementation_steps: Each task has 7-10 actionable steps; no task under 2 steps
checks.constraint_compliance: Pre-locked schemas honored (no api-contracts.ts edits), Q2 late-bind enforced (no id frontmatter), Cursor-OOS documented as schema deviation, vitest.config untouched, single .cjs script across clients, stdout JSON output (NOT stderr+exit2), .codex/hooks.json (NOT .toml), per-task atomic commits with conventional style
